name: Algarvio Portuguese
notes: Algarvio Portuguese, southernmost continental dialect (Faro, Portimão, Quarteira, Lagos). Categorical features per DIALECT_PATTERNS / whitepaper5 (TigreGotico internal dialect research): (1) /ej/ → [e] MONOPHTHONG (Alentejo-like); (2) /ow/ → [o] MONOPHTHONG; (3) SIBILANT VOICING — word-final /s/ → [ʒ] (mas a→[maʒ ɐ], muitos amigos→[ˈmũj̃tuʒ ɐˈmiɡuʒ]); encoded in positional_graphemes s/word_final→[ʒ], coda→[ʒ]; this is a distinct feature from Alentejo (which uses s→z cross-word sandhi); (4) nasal -em endings: /ɐ̃j̃/ → [ẽ] class (shared with Alentejo); (5) open vowel qualities [ɛ, ɔ]; (6) no betacism, /v/ ≠ /b/. NOTES-ONLY: apico-alveolar sibilant tendency (older/rural speakers) not encoded. Historical Mozarabic/Arabic prosodic features are suprasegmental.

ancestors.notes: Standard European Portuguese; Algarvio diverges in sibilant voicing (s→ʒ coda/final), ei/ou monophthongs, and open vowel qualities

sources.author: Boléo, M.P.
sources.title: Estudos de linguística portuguesa e românica

sources.author: TigreGotico
sources.title: DIALECT_PATTERNS.md and whitepaper5: Phoneme-Level Dialect Transforms for European Portuguese
sources.notes: Internal dialect feature matrix and IPA transform inventory; pending external peer review

stress.notes: Acordo Ortográfico 1990 conventions: paroxytone default; written accents override; final -i/-u/-r/-l/-z/-x/-im/-om/-um/-ns and nasal diphthongs attract final stress. Unmarked -em/-am stay paroxytone (homem, falam).
